title: verification_runs Table and Ephemeral Fly Verification Runner

story.as_a: tenant operator needing independent proof that a story's tests actually pass on the post-commit tree
story.i_want_to: have loopctl spawn an ephemeral Fly.io machine at verification time, fetch the commit SHA from the project's git remote, run the test suite and the per-AC verification checks in a clean container, and report results back
story.so_that: the implementer cannot lie about test results because the verification environment is one it never touched

rationale: The independent re-execution layer (section 3 L3) requires a process the implementer cannot reach. Fly.io ephemeral machines are the cheapest way to get that isolation without standing up separate infrastructure. This story ships the runner — Phase 4's GitHub Actions integration (US-26.4.3) is the faster path when CI already exists; the Fly runner is the fallback.

acceptance_criteria.description: Migration creates `verification_runs` table: id (uuid PK), tenant_id (FK), story_id (FK), commit_sha (text), commit_content_hash (bytea, SHA-256 of the tree), status (enum: pending/running/pass/fail/error), started_at, completed_at, runner_type (enum: ci_github/ci_gitlab/fly_machine), ac_results (jsonb: map of ac_id → {status, evidence}), logs_url (text), machine_id (text, Fly machine id when applicable).

`Loopctl.Workers.VerificationRunner` Oban worker processes jobs from a dedicated `:verification` queue. On dequeue it: (a) fetches the commit SHA from the project's git remote using libgit2 or a `git fetch` + `git show` subprocess; (b) computes the tree content hash; (c) dispatches to the appropriate runner based on project.ci_provider.

For `ci_provider = :none` or as the fallback when CI results are unavailable, the worker creates an ephemeral Fly machine via the Fly GraphQL API. The machine boots a project-appropriate image (Elixir, Node.js, Python) with the commit SHA checked out, runs `mix test` (or equivalent), captures stdout/stderr, and reports results back to loopctl before self-destructing.

The Fly machine runs with a short-lived auth token that can ONLY call the verification-result reporting endpoint on loopctl. It cannot read tenant data, cannot query other stories, cannot write to the audit chain directly. The token expires on machine destruction.

Per-AC verification: for each AC with `type: test`, the runner greps the test output for the named test and records pass/fail. For `type: code`, it greps the specified file region against the pattern. For `type: route`, it compile-checks the router. For `type: migration`, it parses migration files. For `type: manual`, it marks the AC as `manual_review_pending`.
Commit verification: loopctl computes the sha256 of the fetched tree contents and stores it. Subsequent lookups of the same commit return the stored hash without re-fetching.
Rate limiting: per-tenant cap of 50 verification runs per day by default, configurable. Exceeding the cap queues runs with a delay instead of failing them.
Failed runs retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff. Persistent failures mark the run as `error` and alert the operator via webhook.
Logs: the Fly machine streams output to an S3-compatible object store (Fly's own storage, not a new vendor), and loopctl stores the URL. The operator dashboard from US-26.4.4 links to the logs.
Tenant isolation: each run is scoped to one tenant; the Fly machine's short-lived token is bound to the tenant_id and cannot call loopctl endpoints for other tenants.
OpenAPI updates, CHANGELOG entry, deployment notes for Fly machine templates and API token setup.

test_cases.name: Worker enqueued, fetches commit, runs tests on a stub Fly runner
preconditions: Mock Fly adapter captures machine lifecycle calls
Story with a test-type AC and a fetchable commit SHA
expected_results: Mock adapter confirms machine creation and destruction

test_cases.name: Non-existent commit SHA marks run as error with retry
steps: Enqueue run with a bogus SHA

test_cases.name: Tenant isolation: ephemeral machine's token cannot access other tenants
preconditions: Mock the machine's outbound API call
steps: Machine attempts to call an endpoint for a different tenant_id

technical_notes: Fly GraphQL API: use a Req-based client with a FLY_API_TOKEN from Fly secrets.
Machine images: pre-built per language, published under loopctl's Fly registry. Build once, reuse.
Short-lived token: a scoped capability token (reuse Capabilities.mint from US-26.3.1) valid for the machine's lifetime, type `verification_report_cap`.
Rate limiting: use the existing Hammer or ETS-based rate limiter from earlier epics.
libgit2 binding: use the `:git` hex package or shell out to git via System.cmd/2 in a sandboxed tmpdir.
